name: Crib
description: This is the foundational stage where learners are introduced to digital literacy through play-based learning. Children engage with visual, interactive tools (like block-based coding, games, and simple robotics concepts) to build curiosity, basic logic, and familiarity with technology.

name: Clicker
description: At this stage, learners transition from guided play to structured creation. They begin working on projects, learning actual programming concepts, and using beginner-friendly languages. Focus is on building problem-solving skills, logical thinking, and creating simple applications or games.

name: Quest
description: This is the advanced stage where learners dive deeper into real-world tech skills such as programming, AI, robotics, and software development. Emphasis is on complex projects, critical thinking, innovation, and preparing learners for future academic or career paths in technology.
